title: UG POL3226 Research Essay - Campaign Contributions and Congressional Voting Behvaviour in the US
text:
This is an undergraduate paper I wrote for professor Amy McKay’s class on Money, Lobbying and Policymaking. More specifically, it looks to test whether pharmaceutical PAC campaign contributions had influenced Congressional voting on insulin pricing reforms included in the 2022 Inflation Reduction Act.
Here, I build my own dataset on
This paper received a grade of 80/100.

title: Home
text:
I am a first year student of the Master in Economics at SciencesPo, Paris.
This website is currently under construction! Thank you for your patience as I set it up. It’s goal will be to share any past and ongoing projects of mine.

title: Paper Presentation - MPCs, MPEs, and Multipliers: A Trilemma for New Keynesian Models by Adrien Auclert, Bence Bardóczy, and Matthew Rognlie
text:
Hello, we are Guillaume, Joanna, and Lorenz. This is the website page for our Macroeconomics 2 presentation on the paper Adrien Auclert, Bence Bardóczy, Matthew Rognlie; MPCs, MPEs, and Multipliers: A Trilemma for New Keynesian Models. The Review of Economics and Statistics 2023; 105 (3): 700–712.
Here are our ressources:

Link to the 2023 paper
PDF of the 2021 paper
Presentation Slides
Slides Latec Code

This presentation was assessed by teaching assistant Naomi Cohen and received a grade of 18/20.

title: Strategic Interactions in Foreign Aid Games - Pousse, Pereira, Sharma (2024)
text:
As part of Emeric Henry’s second semester Microeconomics class on Game Theory, students are required to co-write a paper on a related topic of choice.
Myself, as well as classmates Rodrigo Pereira and Vivan Sharma, chose to study strategic interactions in foreign aid.
Please find below:

Strategic Interactions in Foreign Aid Games - Pousse, Pereira, Sharma (2024)
The Latex code

This paper received the grade of 15.5/20.
